name: Qat
description: Qat is a central figure in the mythology of the Banks Islands and northern Vanuatu, particularly among the people of the New Hebrides. He is a culture hero, trickster, and creator deity, often depicted as a benevolent figure who brought civilization and order to the world. Qat is not a supreme creator in the absolute sense but rather a shaper and transformer of pre-existing chaos. He is credited with creating humans (sometimes from trees or stones), introducing agriculture, teaching crafts, and establishing social customs. He is often accompanied by his brothers, Tangaro and Marawa, who assist him in his endeavors. Qat's stories are filled with humor and cunning, as he frequently outwits malevolent spirits and monsters. He is associated with light, knowledge, and progress, contrasting with the forces of darkness and ignorance. While primarily benevolent, Qat can also be mischievous and prone to errors, adding a human dimension to his divine character. His actions are often presented as teaching moments, demonstrating the importance of wisdom, cooperation, and respect for the natural world. Qat's influence extends beyond the material realm, as he is also connected to the spiritual world and the ancestors. He serves as a bridge between the living and the dead, ensuring the continuity of traditions and the well-being of the community. Stories of Qat are central to the cultural identity of the people who revere him, providing a framework for understanding their origins, values, and place in the cosmos. He is a complex and multifaceted deity, embodying both the creative and destructive forces that shape the world.
subtitle: Culture Hero, Creator, and Trickster

keyMyths.title: The Creation of Humanity
keyMyths.description: Qat carved the first humans from trees (or sometimes stones), bringing them to life and teaching them how to survive. This myth explains the origin of human life and Qat's role as a benefactor.

keyMyths.title: Qat and the First Fire
keyMyths.description: Qat stole fire from a powerful spirit, bringing it to humanity and enabling cooking and warmth. This story highlights Qat's cunning and his commitment to improving human life.
keyMyths.source: Deacon, A.B. (1927). Malekula: A Vanishing People in the New Hebrides

keyMyths.title: Qat's Defeat of the Monster
keyMyths.description: Qat outwitted a fearsome monster that threatened the people, using his intelligence and trickery to save them. This tale demonstrates Qat's role as a protector and champion of the weak.
keyMyths.source: Layard, J. (1942). Stone Men of Malekula

sources.author: Codrington, R.H.
